mnemonics: HEAPSORT: Heapify Everything, Arrange Perfectly, Sort Order Reached Timely
keyPoints: A heap is a complete binary tree where each node is greater than or equal to its children (max-heap) or less than or equal to its children (min-heap)
Heapsort uses a max-heap to repeatedly extract the maximum element and place it at the end of the array
Building a heap from an unordered array takes O(n) time using a bottom-up approach, while heapsort overall takes O(n log n) time
commonMistakes: Confusing heap properties with binary search tree properties (BST has left < parent < right, heap has parent > both children)
Forgetting to maintain the heap property after swapping elements during the extraction phase
Using the wrong index calculations for parent/child nodes (parent = (i-1)/2, left child = 2i+1, right child = 2i+2)
